name: Ali Kordan Impeachment
description: On 4 November 2008, the [Iranian parliament](/orgs/islamic-consultative-assembly) voted to impeach Interior Minister Ali Kordan after just 90 days in office. Kordan, appointed by President [Mahmoud Ahmadinejad](/people/mahmoud-ahmadinejad), had claimed to hold an honorary doctorate in law from the University of Oxford. When challenged, he produced a forged diploma. Oxford University confirmed that no such degree had ever been awarded. Kordan subsequently admitted in a letter to Ahmadinejad that he had been 'deceived by a person who claimed to represent Oxford University in Tehran.' The impeachment was a rare instance of the parliament successfully removing a sitting minister and was widely seen as a blow to Ahmadinejad's credibility.

sources.title: Iran minister impeached over faked degree
Iran minister fired over fake PhD

editorial_notes: Kordan confirmed by parliament 169-100 in August 2008. Impeached November 2008. He later claimed innocence, saying he was duped.
